name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in DOE-guided process development, hypothesis-driven experimental design, analytical characterization, and scale-up-oriented technical reporting. Experienced developing process windows, analyzing out-of-trend data, and translating multi-technique results into practical next-step recommendations for cross-functional development teams.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead chemical process development and optimization for bio-based polymer systems, using DOE to identify critical variables, improve yield/quality, and define reproducible process specifications for scale-up.
Analyze analytical, thermal, and process data to recommend next-step experiments, operating ranges, and material-quality controls.
Designed and executed hypothesis-driven experiments across 15+ material variants, interpreting performance and degradation data to propose next-step design and process decisions.
Authored clear technical conclusions from complex multi-technique datasets for multidisciplinary research and development stakeholders.
Co-led thin-film process development for defense-oriented coatings, establishing reproducible deposition strategies and practical process-control parameters in a demanding R&D setting.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes with DOE and in-situ monitoring, improving process understanding and reproducibility.
Managed operations for a 15-member research group, coordinated instrument planning and SOP-style documentation, and improved instrument uptime by 30%.

leadership.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
bullets: Mentored 120+ undergraduates and developed supplemental materials that improved exam performance by 15%, strengthening technical communication and training skills.

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Process
projects.description: Leading DOE-guided process optimization and characterization studies that define reproducible process windows and quality-relevant specifications for polymer systems moving toward scale-up.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Developed and refined deposition processes for thin-film coatings, balancing experimental execution, data analysis, and practical process control decisions.
projects.tech: Thin films, process development, experimental design

projects.title: Electrochromic Material Reliability Study
projects.description: Linked formulation-adjacent design choices with material performance using structured experimental plans and multi-technique characterization.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, TGA, DSC, data analysis

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Process Development: DOE, process optimization, protocol development, scale-up specifications, workplans, reproducibility studies, technical reporting
skills.Analytical Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, LC-MS, GC-MS, NMR, TGA, DSC, GPC/SEC, ellipsometry, QCM-D, cyclic voltammetry
skills.Data & Quality Methods: SPC (Coursera 2026), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), out-of-trend investigation, documentation, SOP-ready controls
skills.Materials & Product Development: Polymers, thin films, structure-property-processing relationships, stability-sensitive materials, product-performance evaluation
skills.Project Execution: Cross-functional communication, external-partner-ready technical summaries, multi-project prioritization, laboratory coordination

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award
